note_on_methodology_bug: an earlier n=100 comparison used mate_score=100000 in acpl_probe.py, which let rare forced-mate-in-N detections dominate the MEAN with near-lottery-sized outliers (a single such position could add ~250cp to the average at n=400). Caught by re-running at larger n and seeing ACPL jump from ~300 to ~1000-1600 -- fixed by capping mate_score at 1000 (standard ACPL convention), matching common chess-analysis tooling.
conclusion: outcome-conditioned training (--winner-pov-only) produces a real, statistically significant improvement in tactical safety BEYOND what the same number of additional plain-continuation training steps produces. Effect size is modest (~22cp mean reduction vs the step-matched control) but the paired test is unambiguous (p=0.0046, 95% CI excludes 0). Adopting --winner-pov-only as the default going forward; combining it with the quasimetric embedding swap next.
